mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market cooling that reduces wage inflation pressures, prompting markets to price in imminent monetary easing and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns fail to materialize in > 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening economic output that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the consumer price index signals disinflation that removes tail risks to central bank policy, allowing equity markets to reprice higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain negative or flat in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL shows month-over-month declines.

mechanism_narrative: Weak nonfarm payrolls growth combined with rising unemployment rates indicates labor market deterioration that forecasts recessionary conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of weak PAYEMS and rising UNRATE yields positive 60-day SPX returns in > 58% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal consumption expenditures reflect robust household spending that supports near-term corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS predicting 5-day SPX direction falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals supply-side weakness that triggers risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines exceed 0.5% month-over-month.
